title: Add attachments to email integrations
excerpt: We want to embed a Monday.com form into our website, so that visitors to the site can leave their details and sign up for a free worksheet to download. We’d like to do it through an automated email integration in Monday.…

posts.author: AndyClayton (Andy Clayton)
posts.cookedHtml:
We want to embed a Monday.com form into our website, so that visitors to the site can leave their details and sign up for a free worksheet to download. We’d like to do it through an automated email integration in Monday.com (using Outlook integration), however currently it is not supported to send attachments in an email integration.

Could this be added please?

Thanks

Andy

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 On this, i have been requesting this feature since past year.
Is the only thing its lacking in order to send our technical reports to our clients.

posts.author: jason.clark (jason clark)
posts.cookedHtml:
Andy, I was testing the idea of sending our customers a PDF proof of their job request through an integrated setup. I decided against it but it may help you. We are a print shop and use each task or row as a job in the shop. Works beautifully.

What if you add a file cell column to your group, including the file you want to email in that cell, have an integration create an email to your customer using that file cell as an attachment? It may not be truly automatic but require some status change after the fact, to fire off the email with attachment. What I call “semi automatic”. Just an idea…

posts.author: AndyClayton (Andy Clayton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks Jason. In just tried it, and it does work, thanks for the tip.
However, there’s a few issues:

  • the attachment needs to be uploaded to each pulse, similar to attaching a document to each email.
  • the email goes out with a link to the attachment, which the recipient then has to click on to download
  • As you say, the each pulse needs to have status changed one by one.

The biggest issue is the first one, uploading the attachment line by line. Any ideas on how that could be fixed?

Thanks for the help man.

Andy

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
We need this too…
To send attached files in gmail integrations its a basic feature that needs to be fixed.

posts.author: jason.clark (jason clark)
posts.cookedHtml:
@AndyClayton those are tough drawbacks. In our situation, the PDF was created art so it was original to each pulse and could be dropped in by the designers, as created. I’ll try and keep your situation in mind.

I haven’t tested any Google Drive or similar integration but I wonder if you couldn’t do an integration that would trigger an email and include a file from Drive. That’s where your file could be stored instead of a pulse cell specifically.

You are welcome for the help, only wish it helped more.

posts.author: jason.clark (jason clark)
posts.cookedHtml: @AndyClayton I just thought of another workaround. I don’t personally use mailchimp any more but maybe when they sign up on the form, it integrates with mailchimp to create a contact (not a bad thing on its own), then mailchimp can shoot out a welcome email with attached worksheet. Maybe two birds with one stone…

posts.author: AndyClayton (Andy Clayton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks Jason. Our solution is to use Mailchimp, to keep the full ‘master list’ there, and then set an integration only for those that request a second follow up, who get put into a Monday.com list for more personalised follow up.

I like your suggestions, though it would mean two identical, duplicate lists, one in Monday, one in mailchimp, and that sort of duplication always makes me nervous.

I appreciate all the cool suggestions! I’m new to this community, and it’s great to see others into Monday.com solutions

posts.author: jason.clark (jason clark)
posts.cookedHtml: @AndyClayton, good you have a workaround anyway. I’m new to this as well, since November. I’m building new uses, itegrations and automations pretty regular. Great tool.

posts.author: snicho (Stephen Nicholson)
posts.cookedHtml:
We have a similar use case where we need field staff to take some photos and add them to a form, complete other relevant fields, and then have the form emailed to the email address that is one of the fields in the form.

Right now we are doing this, and more, by using a custom html form that sits on another site. We create a pdf of the completed form, including the attached images, email the pdf to the email address that was included in the email field on the form, then use Integromat to transfer the data to a monday board, and add attachments as an update against the newly created item.

Once it is set up, it works ok. We are even creating dropdown fields on the form from data (items) that are contained on other boards in monday; which is really powerful. But it takes a lot of time to set up.

If we could use either the Outlook or Gmail integration to email the photos that are attached to a monday form, it would save us many hours of work.

posts.author: Anderson (Anderson Schulle)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 on this feature. Ideally just attaching the files from filetype column to the email as an actual attachment.
Or a second way to make it work is if the monday links to those files would be accessible to people without a monday account.

Our scenario is that we send owners and tenants documents via email. We can automate every part of different workflows, except for the attachment of files part.

So we have to manually put files in a secondary location (cloud), make them shareable, get the link, and put them inside monday to send that link to owners and tenants.

Would really, really like to see a solution implemented so that we can make full use of our workflows. I assume others would have similar case use for this as well?

Would be curious to hear where this is in the development pipeline.

posts.author: xxlawdog1xx (Michael Tillman)
posts.cookedHtml: I am looking for similar solution too. I need the attachment contained within the “file” column of the board to be included with automated E-Mail sent.

posts.author: Yu1992 (Yumi)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 same here!!! Would be very helpful to be able to attach a file

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Ronk Is this in the roadmap for the improved email features / CRM solution?

Also being discussed here in the context of the API as well - Email integration discards attachments? - #11 by Anderson

posts.author: jlaroche (Jean LaRoche)
posts.cookedHtml: We generate PDF (invoices, certificates, 1 pager-report about one item) with a single-click integration with Eledo. It works perfectly. Quite elegant, in fact … but… no email integration to actually send that file to anyone. It’s a bugger…

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: Another BIG problem is that to access the link and download the file you have to be previously logged in Monday.com… anyone notice that??

posts.author: ilia510 (Ilia Zelenkin)
posts.cookedHtml:
We’ve recently been working with client with a similar issue. We’ve changed the process in a way that now engineers loads the finished work to a Sharepoint folder where integromat detects the change, creates the tasks with Link to files (a task and subitem per file), we get the data out, populate metadata of the task and once it is done, a deliverable is generated from the task and emailed to the customer with a link instead of a file, and then task is moved to Billing board.

In a nutshell, we’ve avoided Files column due to mentioned concerns and giving a client more file permission control over who downloads what.

Hopefully, it will help somebody. Feel free to ping me for details.

posts.author: alberto.torres.net (Alberto Torres)
posts.cookedHtml: This really needs to be accounted for, since its a really big set back when using the platform, specially on Finance reports and invoices. The file column should be able to attach files or links to files without requiring or demanding the login process. We understand perfectly this is part of the security on monday.com but we really need it to be available on notifications and on email integrations, specially since the onedrive integration simply drops the link away when sending the email.

posts.author: Clarke.Roberts (Clarke Roberts)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would also find this incredibly useful!

I want to use a form as a way of accepting work requests from other departments whose members aren’t on Monday. Currently, I’m using email integrations/automations to send emails to people who submit work requests, providing them updates on the status of their request but also want to send drafted documents for feedback via an email automation, based on a file that is uploaded to the item on a board.

Currently, the only way to do this is to have Monday send a link to the uploaded file, but that requires people to have a Monday account to view it, which we don’t want.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, this is a so many requested feature.
Can anyone from Monday Team provide any information about?
We have at least 2 boards on standby because of this.

posts.author: Admeirel (Adolpho Meirelles)
posts.cookedHtml:
Also needing this feature here!
It would be really useful to solve some points in our workflow to be able to annex documents to automatic e-mails.

posts.author: Graham_Swann (Graham Swann)
posts.cookedHtml: This is so really needed. Pleaase integrate this ASAP.

posts.author: Arthur_Hooly (Arthur)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m looking for this also, need this ASAP

posts.author: dan-eland (Dan Eland)
posts.cookedHtml: I just created an email automation to send the client a proposal document as an attachment, but they have to log in to download it. Can you make this optional, or a little more intuitive for whoever clicks the link?

posts.author: Dcrum (Daniel Crum)
posts.cookedHtml:
@monday-team Can any update be provided on this?

There needs to be a simple resolution to allow files to download from monday to non-monday accounts.

posts.author: MPickens (Michael Pickens)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be a great addition. As a temporary workaround, I send a webhook to integromat, grab the file, and attach it inside gmail… however… the file that is attached inside the email is corrupted (I can still view it from the board just fine) - any one know anything about this? The file im trying to attach is docx.

posts.author: michaelmhughes (Michael Hughes)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 to this suggestion as well. Hope we can get it in 2022!

posts.author: mzingales (Matteo Zingales)
posts.cookedHtml: On the same page with @dan-eland. Allowing PDFs to be opened through a link without having to login to the Monday.com platform is essential for our company sending invoices/ receipts / etc. to our clients. Hoping that this can be resolved.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Julia-monday.com hi!
Can someone please update this?
We need to know how far is this to be implemented.

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
@hlopezvc Hey,

Is your use case specifically using automation to send emails which you need to contain attachments? I ask because I know sending emails using the Emails & Activities feature allows for attachments to be included but not with emails which are automated.

Cheers,
Julia

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Julia-monday.com thanks for your response.
My use case here is very simple, we have pulses with some equipment maintenance information that is some kind of work order. Every time that a task is done we are now creating an integration via Eledo PDF that creates a PDF with all pulse information (As a form) and this PDF goes to a files column. In another column i have the customer email. So with a button i would like to create an automation that sends the generated PDF file directly to the customer email, with some text for introduction.

With that being said, can i do this via email & activities feature? The attachment process needs to be automated to retrieve the file from the file column where it was uploaded via Eledo App.

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
@hlopezvc
Right now it’s not possible but I did share this feedback directly with the product managers who work with the email and activities feature. I have a workaround if the pdf can also be stored outside of monday.com but appear as a link in your board using a link column. Then you can use the email integration to send the email automatically with a link to the file.

Thanks for continuing to share your feedback!

posts.author: MissMonday (Claudia Wengle)
posts.cookedHtml:
We have the same issue,

It would be great if a file I add in a file column, could be attached to the mail just as a regular file attachment, rather than a link.

We manage our courses through Monday, and I created an integration that sends out an email to the student, that includes their certificate from the file column, once I set the status to “Send certificate”

But since it is only a link, people from outside our company can not access the link.
I do not want to make it a public board, because there is sensitive client data stored there.

We are just recently moving all our processes to Monday.com, since our programming and automating crack is no longer with us, and we wanted to try and streamline our multitude of portals and tools as much as possible.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Julia-monday.com how are you.
Could this thread be answered by a Monday team member?
A few years ago i have posted about this, and we still the same because there is NO way or alternative WAY to make this simple. A few years ago here in Argentina this feature was an amazing add on for our customers, and everyone was looking for this. Today we cannot make our customers happy about sending our digital reports in PDF directly via email instead of sending a download link that forces the user to register in monday.

Today here in Argentina, this feature is reachable on any simple app, example last day i got a PDF of my vehicle fleet service provider via whatsapp. (Integrating whatsapp also is not reachable via monday).

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @hlopezvc, thanks for the follow-up!

Did you see my response from February? Add attachments to email integrations - #32 by Julia-monday.com

Right now, I don’t have any further update but the team have seen the feedback and we will update if anything does change

Thank you

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi thanks @Julia-monday.com …
Surely i missed your answer.
Just want to say that this feature Is commonly found in any system or cloud service.

I hope this feedback could bring some priority to this feature.

posts.author: Chaya (Chaya)
posts.cookedHtml: @jason.clark have you found a solution that works for your company? we have a similar dilemma, we provide print and design services and need to send attachment PDFs in just about every client email. as we have thousands, I cannot invite them all to Monday.com…unfortunately, the integration does not work for us, as our Outlook status does not support the necessary licensing…would love some assistance, before we ultimately stop using Monday.com because of this key disruption to our workflow (and I really want to stay, I love it here!!)

posts.author: MichaelSafetek (Michael)

Should I continue investing time (and licensing) to migrate the company workflow to monday, or should I look elsewhere if e-mail attachments (or a public available link to a file) is a deal breaker?

posts.author: InsulationGod (Joshua Friddle)

posts.author: Sash (Sash Margrie hunt)

posts.author: Sunshine (James)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey,
We have 2 different boards, one for CRM and another for Admin.
Our sales person attaches files on the CRM board and once a status is updated to “Sold”, all the data from the CRM board is moved to the Admin board for an administrator to process. I am unable to find a way to move the files from the CRM board to the Admin board. as seen below

link21023×736 38.3 KB

Furthermore when an automated email is created the files are attached as links that only a monday.com user can access as seen below:

link1518×571 45.1 KB

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: We share this problem too, we need a Monday.com native solution for this…

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Today, any web platform system has the ability to send pdf files generated inside the platform, credit card info, service invoices, web shop invoices, etc etc.
How come this cannot be done?

We are tryin from more than a year this, to generate pdf files via Eledo PDF and send them via gmail integration, as easy as this… and send our customer their files from each pulse. And we cant

Its a very basic need, in order to do professional boards.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all,

Firstly, we are really sorry for the delay in responding to this thread! We are working on improving our responsiveness to the community (we promise!), particularly our feature request process so appreciate your patience in advance!

On that note, as we pull together all the votes associated with this topic, we would love if you can redirect the votes on this thread, to the primary thread (most voted) here: Add attachments to email integrations - #48 by Alex5 - as we plan to close this smaller thread in an attempt to consolidate all votes, and better prioritise this feature request based upon the total amount of votes

Thanks!

posts.author: msampson (Marty Sampson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Here’s the response I got from Monday.com

Amelia Orellana (monday.com)
Feb 7, 2023, 12:39 PM CST
Ahh, thank you so much for clarifying this for me, Marty!

I’d like to apologize for the delay in our response time, we’ve been receiving a higher volume of requests than usual and are just now able to get back to you but not to worry, I’m here and happy to help!

I understand that you’re looking to have files attached to emails that are logged in the update section of the item when the Outlook integration is triggered! While we realize how helpful this would be to your workflow, at the moment we do not support that functionality.

Please know that our product team is eager for this kind of feedback to include in their discussions for future improvements so we’ll be sure to share how we can improve the Outlook integration with them While we can’t offer an ETA for you at the moment, keep an eye out in the near future as we add new and exciting features to the platform!

Let me know if you have any questions in the meantime - I’m more than happy to help!

Best,
Amelia

posts.author: lblblb (Luke Bills)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1
We’d really appreciate this functionality. Have hit a roadblock in one of our workflows because of it.

posts.author: LukeSellers (Luke Sellers)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 This has absolutely got to be the most requested feature I have personally seen on the Monday.com community page. I cannot fathom how this has not been prioritized for work yet lol. Every single user of this platform is most likely going to run into this issue at some point or another, and only the ones savvy with other no code solutions and integrations will be able to find feasible workarounds. Please Monday, let this be a native feature so I can send my email attachments without having to give Guest access to random Vendors!

posts.author: ggrimaldo (Giselle)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 to this! Would love to be able to share files as attachments via e-mail from a file column even when the recipient does not have a monday.com account.

Beyond that, would be helpful if the files column had the option to auto-populate with a template file when a new item is created. Feels too repetitive to attach a “doc” > “doc templates” and select the template every time when I know I’m using the same exact template for every row item created and just filling in specific project information.

posts.author: ToriG (Vitoria Gray)
posts.cookedHtml:
This year we used Monday to send out our employee goals via email. The goals are in a document file, and so we figured that if we set up an email automation the file would attach itself as a document. What we found is that the document attached itself as a link.

This issue with this is that the employee wouldn’t be able to view the document without requesting access, whereas if it were an attachment, they would’ve been able to see the document.

We were able to problem solve this by using the dropbox attachment, which allows for the employee to simply view the document.

If possible it would be nice if Monday email automation was able to find a way to have the file attach rather than be a link!

posts.author: Hezibo (Hezi Bosmi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @ToriG
Have you managed to solve this issue? I’m facing it as well
Thx

posts.author: ToriG (Vitoria Gray)
posts.cookedHtml: I belive the dropbox solution I mentioned is the only way right now, I haven’t seen this solved yet.

posts.author: Hezibo (Hezi Bosmi)
posts.cookedHtml:
It’s a pity.
Thank you for your response

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey! Thank you for this feedback. We sincerely apologise for the delay in responding. Our team is working on improving our response process, so appreciate your understanding in advance

That being said, currently this is a setback that our team is aware of and hoping to address at some point in the future, although we are unable to provide a specific eta as of now. As an alternative, can I kindly ask if you had come across our emails and activities app which allows you to attach files directly from the files column?

  • Emails & Activities on monday.com

Thank you!
Ashley

posts.author: SevenDayPermits (Rob)
posts.cookedHtml: I created a bunch of automations to send emails with PDF’s clients can fill out. Unfortunately it looks like Monday does not allow this - you need to be a user of Monday. So if my team receives these emails, they can simply download the link. But everyone not in the team (a new client for example) the link takes them to monday.com to sign in. I do not want to have hundreds of guest accounts, I just want to be able to send PDF’s via an automated email. This would be an amazing feature if that could be added

posts.author: Cyrille (L)
posts.cookedHtml: @Rob We stopped bothering with monday email automations for anything sent to clients. We use zapier to send the email with the gmail or outlook steps that is triggered by a change of status in Monday. If you use outlook in zapier, at the moment you can send only 1 attachement and with gmail as many as you want with a limit of 25mg total. So we created a personal gmail account with a redirect to our email address, and in the automation, we specify to reply to the desired email address. It works pretty well.

posts.author: SevenDayPermits (Rob)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for the info! I will check this out this weekend and see if I can make that work for me!

posts.author: Jakesmonday (Jake)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Cyrille This sounds like a great solution.

Are you using this for emails that have the same attachment going out time and time again?

What about if you need an attachment to go out that is unique to the recipient like a contract for example?

posts.author: SevenDayPermits (Rob)
posts.cookedHtml: @Cyrille and @Jakesmonday I tried messing around with Zapier. I found it to be a little cumbersome and just extra steps for something that should be so simple haha - I really do not need another “app”. I instead decided to update the automation and simply link to a Dropbox link. All the attachments I need to send are typical (application forms, authorizations, info sheets, etc.) things I need my clients to fill out and return. So I created a Dropbox folder with all the documents I have, updated dropbox so anyone with the link can view/download but can not edit/delete and simply added the link to the automation. Would be nice to save my clients an extra step and just be able to add a PDF through Monday, but everything works perfect linking to Dropbox, now I can still automate these emails, and send PDF’s, it simply sends the URL to a dropbox folder where the files are. Tested it and all really seamless.

posts.author: Cyrille (L)
posts.cookedHtml: @Jacques you can do both. We use it for the same file in all emails that is fetched from a google drive. But you could programm the zap to read a file from a monday column instead. Zapier has a large user forum like Monday. Hope that helps!

posts.author: Jakesmonday (Jake)
posts.cookedHtml:
@SevenDayPermits Thanks for the feedback Rob. I also do this using google drive (with the folder open to anyone with the link)

It works and we currently use it for some of our workflows. That said the clients expect an attachment as this is the industry standard and ultimately thats where I am trying to go.

I will explore the option of firing of an email based on a file in a column.

Appreciate that option though and will keep it on hand as an alternative.

Cheers.

posts.author: Jakesmonday (Jake)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks @Cyrille, that option to pull it from a column sounds like a solution.

Just another question, the emails you send out are they all templated emails or do you also use this when sending out a custom email to a client?

posts.cookedHtml: This is a serieus shortcoming, not able to automate workflows that involve our clients.

posts.author: shane.hairston (Shane Hairston)
posts.cookedHtml: For our use case, we use Outlook. This integration works except that it doesn’t support file attachments. It would be nice to get an update with a more specific timeframe - even if it is a rough estimate.

posts.author: trinja (Trinity Doyle)
posts.cookedHtml: A timeframe would be great! Especially since the offered work around of using the emails & activities app is now a paid feature.

posts.author: ArianeLW (Ariane)
posts.cookedHtml: Also keen to have an estimated timeline on this!

posts.author: dharmikjagodana (DHARMIK JAGODANA)
posts.cookedHtml: Consider EasyMails for your email integration needs. With EasyMails, you can automate the process of sending emails with attachments directly from Monday.com.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Everyone, just spoke with the team and due to other responsibilities and priorities, the team is not currently working on the development of the feature. While there is no definite timeline for this feature, it is something that the team is planning to get to in the future. The team mentioned that they would love to hear more feedback to help them decide when to prioritize this feature.

In the meantime, there are some great apps that can help you with this and you can look through the thread for suggested apps from our community.

posts.author: shayan.moussawi (Shayan Moussawi)
posts.cookedHtml: I am really surprised this isn’t a priority. I was hoping this would come along with the CRM sequences feature that is in the Pipeline. As I would expect to send HTML Formatted Emails to my customers in sequences. Sending plain Text Emails is really something of the past to be honest.

posts.cookedHtml: Yes, please. We really need this feature for our form submission confirmation email. posts.author: christinap (Christina Punchard)

What a shame it’s not a priority. We desperately need this but we are not authorised to spend anymore money buying apps, especially when it’s something that the tool should provide.
It isn’t really acceptable for you to expect your customers to pay money to third parties for basic features, rather than fixing it yourself. Wish I’d raised this with Eran at Elevate last week.

posts.author: grandcity (grandcity)
posts.cookedHtml: We really need this feature in work management. When we add a file and set up our email automations, it adds it as a link in the body of the email. We need it as an attachment in the email as the receiving email can only read attachments. Disappointing to see that almost 5 years later and still nothing…

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! Amy here from the monday.com team. We know many of you have been asking for the ability to include attachments in emails sent through our email integrations. While this feature is still in the research phase, we’re happy to share that it’s back on our long-term roadmap and is being planned for 2025.

We appreciate your patience as our team works to explore the best way to bring this functionality to life. Your feedback plays a huge role in shaping our roadmap, so please keep sharing your thoughts with us! Stay tuned for more updates as we make progress.

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! Thank you for your continued feedback around the ability to include attachments in emails sent through our integrations.

We’re happy to share that this feature is now in progress and is currently planned for release in Q3 2025! We’ll be sure to keep you updated as we get closer to release. Thank you again for your input!

posts.author: Shivani (Shivani Chinnappan)
posts.cookedHtml: @amybronstein Has there been any update on this solution? Projected launch date?

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello everyone! We wanted to give you all a quick heads up about a prioritization shift for upcoming feature releases. As part of the ongoing review of our roadmap, we have decided to focus on other capabilities within the Integrations domain for now.

This means that this specific feature will take a bit more time to reach you, but it’s still on our radar, and the team is continuing to make progress behind the scenes! Thanks for your continued patience and understanding. We’ll be sure to share more updates as things evolve!

posts.author: Juan_Barrios_Piaggio (Juan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

New here to the community.

My name is Juan and i have 18+ in the industry. I’m currently in search for solo projects to do and i came across this, which interested me.

Last night i made an mvp of this requirement as a monday app.

Is anyone still interested in this? Should i sync more time into deliverying this to potential customers? I made an mvp using SendGrid, which can utimately have your worksheets been sent frfom you outlook account. i know just having a button that you can sign in straight into you outlook outcome would probably be preffered, but on the development side, that has a cost. So in the spirit of keeping costs low i decided to go with SendGrid.

Thanks for anyone that replies.

Hope everyone is enjoying their Fridays

–Juan

posts.author: SariHoltz (Sari Holtz)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Shivani (and everyone else on this thread!)

You can add attachments to emails and templates in SuperMail.

Try it out - it’s a game changer.

posts.author: adamcarter (Adam )
posts.cookedHtml: Casso .app fixes this in Monday. You can use any email platform, saves your email and replies as comments, attachments are all saved to task, can update existing tasks using task urls, use multiple email threads to one task, updates the attachments in a task with those attachments. No duplicates, zaps, extensions.
